report_name: UK Bill Expands NIS Regulations to Data Centers, Enhances Gov Security Powers

identities.name: United Kingdom Government
identities.description: Introduced the Cyber Security and Resilience (Network and Information Systems) Bill to amend and expand the 2018 NIS Regulations.

identities.name: Data Centers
identities.description: A sector newly brought under the scope of the UK's NIS regulations by the Cyber Security and Resilience Bill.

identities.name: Managed Service Providers (MSPs)
identities.description: A class of third-party IT service providers newly brought under the scope of the UK's NIS regulations by the Cyber Security and Resilience Bill.

identities.name: House of Lords
identities.description: The upper house of the UK Parliament where the Cyber Security and Resilience Bill was introduced in June 2026.

identities.name: House of Commons
identities.description: The lower house of the UK Parliament which the Cyber Security and Resilience Bill must pass through to become law.

identities.name: Information Commissioner's Office
identities.description: Mentioned as an example of a relevant competent authority to which security incidents must be reported under the NIS regulations.

identities.name: National Cyber Security Centre
identities.description: The NCSC's Cyber Assessment Framework (CAF) is cited as the basis for NIS compliance and a starting point for gap analysis.
